stories.title: Knicks Make History — First Team With Three Straight 25-Point Playoff Wins

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: HISTORIC. Knicks became the FIRST TEAM IN NBA HISTORY to win 3 straight playoff games by 25+ points. 119-pt combined margin. Brunson 35. They are not playing around. #HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Embiid: 3-for-11. 14 points. Brunson: 35 points — 27 in the FIRST HALF. The 76ers had no answers and MSG had no mercy. Knicks 137, 76ers 98. #HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The New York Knicks just did something no team has ever done in NBA playoff history.

On Monday night, they dismantled the Philadelphia 76ers 137-98 — winning by 39 points. But the really remarkable part? That was their THIRD consecutive playoff victory by at least 25 points. Three in a row. First time ever.

Jalen Brunson was masterful, scoring 27 of his 35 points in the first half before Philly could even try to make a game of it. OG Anunoby was nearly perfect (7-for-8 from the field, 18 points). Karl-Anthony Towns and Mikal Bridges each added 17.

On the other end, the Knicks held Joel Embiid to 3-for-11 shooting and just 14 points. Tyrese Maxey managed only 13. The team that completed a 3-1 comeback against the defending champions suddenly looks very human against this Knicks machine.

The combined point differential across the Knicks' last three playoff games is 119 points — the largest in any three-game span in postseason history.

We're watching something special unfold at Madison Square Garden. Can anyone stop them?
facebook.image_caption: Knicks 137, 76ers 98. Brunson with 35. Embiid with 14. The Knicks are historically dominant right now — and Game 2 is just around the corner.

article.headline: No Mercy: Knicks Become First Team in NBA History to Win Three Straight Playoff Games by 25+ Points

stories.title: Wembanyama Sets NBA Playoff Blocks Record — But Wolves Steal Game 1

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Wembanyama recorded 12 blocks in Game 1 vs the Wolves. TWELVE. That's the new all-time NBA playoff blocks record. He matched the old record of 10 in THREE QUARTERS. Spurs still lost 104-102. #HoopHeroes #Spurs #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Anthony Edwards was supposed to miss this game. Instead he came off the bench, scored 11 pts in the 4th, and stole a road win from the Spurs — who had a man with 12 blocks on the floor. Wolves 104, Spurs 102. #HoopHeroes #Timberwolves #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Victor Wembanyama put on a defensive performance last night that may never be matched.

In Game 1 of the Western Conference Semifinals in San Antonio, the 22-year-old Spurs center recorded 12 blocks — shattering the all-time NBA playoff record for blocks in a single game. He matched the previous record of 10 in just three quarters.

He also had 11 points, 15 rebounds and 12 blocks — one of the rarest triple-doubles in basketball history, with blocks as the third stat.

The Timberwolves still won, 104-102.

That's where the night gets surreal. Despite Wembanyama's historic effort, Anthony Edwards made sure the storyline wouldn't be clean. Edwards — who was expected to miss the first two games of this series after hyperextending his left knee — came off the bench and scored 18 points, including 11 in the fourth quarter, closing out a narrow Wolves victory.

More blocks than points for Wembanyama. A record-breaking individual game. And still a loss.

The Spurs are still very much alive in this series, but it's a brutal start when your franchise player has arguably the greatest defensive game in playoff history and you come up two points short.
facebook.image_caption: Wembanyama: 11 pts, 15 reb, 12 blocks. The all-time playoff blocks record in a single game — and the Spurs still lost. Anthony Edwards had something to say about that.

article.headline: Block Party, Wrong Result: Wembanyama Shatters NBA Playoff Blocks Record but Wolves Steal Game 1

stories.title: Luka Doncic Ruled Out — Lakers Face Thunder Tonight Without Their Star

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: OFFICIAL: Luka Doncic is OUT for Lakers-Thunder Game 1 tonight. 32+ days since his last game. JJ Redick says he hasn't even started his contact ramp-up yet. LeBron vs Shai — 8:30 PM ET, OKC. Thunder are 15.5-pt favorites. #HoopHeroes #Lakers #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Lakers-Thunder series was already a massive mismatch on paper. Now it's even more lopsided.

Luka Doncic has officially been ruled out for tonight's Game 1 in Oklahoma City, per the official injury report. The Slovenian superstar has been sidelined for more than 32 days with a Grade 2 hamstring strain — and according to his own coach, JJ Redick, he hasn't even begun the contact ramp-up phase of his recovery yet.

"He is not close," Redick said Monday. "There is a whole ramp-up that is required when you are coming back from a hamstring, and he has not started that contact ramp-up yet."

Brian Windhorst of ESPN echoed the concern, warning fans not to expect Luka in the opening games of this series. A potential return window around Game 3 or Game 4 was mentioned, but nothing has been confirmed, and the Lakers are evaluating him on a week-to-week basis.

That leaves LeBron James to take on the Oklahoma City Thunder — the No. 1 seed in the West — as 15.5-point underdogs tonight. The Thunder beat the Lakers by an average of 29.3 points per game during the regular season. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander is playing at an MVP level, and OKC's depth makes them dangerous even when opponents game-plan specifically for their best player.

LeBron James, at this stage of his career, has proven he can carry teams in the playoffs. But keeping pace with a complete, healthy Thunder squad in Oklahoma City — without Doncic drawing defensive attention and creating spacing — is a different kind of challenge. Game time is 8:30 PM ET on NBC/Peacock from Paycom Center.
facebook.image_caption: Luka Doncic: out for Game 1. Lakers are 15.5-point underdogs in OKC tonight. LeBron's going to need a performance for the ages. 8:30 PM ET, NBC.

article.headline: Luka Doncic Ruled Out for Game 1 — Lakers Face Thunder Tonight as 15.5-Point Underdogs

stories.title: Pistons vs Cavaliers Game 1 Tonight — Two Game 7 Warriors Meet

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Two teams. Two Game 7 wins. Zero rest advantage. Pistons host the Cavaliers in Game 1 TONIGHT at 7 PM ET — the battle of the survivors begins. Cade vs Mitchell. Little Caesars Arena is going to be ELECTRIC. #HoopHeroes #Pistons #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
When you look at the 2026 playoffs bracket, the Pistons-Cavaliers series is one of the most emotionally loaded matchups imaginable — and it tips off tonight.

Both teams just survived the most grueling kind of series. Both won Game 7 on May 3 — on the same afternoon. The Pistons completed one of the most stunning comeback stories in recent memory, rallying from a 3-1 deficit to knock out the Magic. The Cavaliers answered with Jarrett Allen's dominant 22-point, 19-rebound performance to eliminate Toronto.

Now these two Game 7 survivors meet in Detroit at 7:00 PM ET for a Round 2 opener with real stakes.

For the Pistons, this is home — Cade Cunningham had 32 points and 12 assists in Game 7, Tobias Harris had 30 (career playoff-high), and the crowd at Little Caesars Arena is going to be loud. For the Cavaliers, this is a road game after an emotional win, with Donovan Mitchell and the gang needing to find that second gear quickly.

Both teams earned this moment the hard way. Tonight we find out who handles it better.
facebook.image_caption: Two Game 7 survivors. One Game 1 tonight. Pistons host the Cavaliers at 7 PM ET — and Little Caesars Arena will be electric after that historic comeback win last weekend.

article.headline: Battle-Tested: Pistons Host Cavaliers in Game 1 After Both Teams Survived Back-to-Back Game 7s

stories.title: Second Round Is Set — Four Series, Four Stories to Watch

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: The 2026 NBA Conference Semis are delivering: Knicks making history. Wemby breaking records. Luka ruled out. Wolves stealing one in SA. Two more Game 1s TONIGHT. Best time of the NBA year. #HoopHeroes #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The 2026 NBA Conference Semifinals are two days old, and they've already given us more drama than most postseasons do in two rounds.

The New York Knicks crushed the 76ers by 39 points Monday night — becoming the first team in NBA history to win three straight playoff games by 25 or more points. Victor Wembanyama set the all-time single-game playoff blocks record with 12 and still watched his Spurs lose by two. Anthony Edwards returned from a knee injury ahead of schedule to steal a road win. Luka Doncic was officially ruled out for tonight's opener in Oklahoma City.

Tonight brings two more Game 1s: the Cavaliers travel to Detroit to face the Pistons at 7 PM ET, and the Lakers travel to OKC to face the Thunder at 8:30 PM ET — with LeBron James leading a 15.5-point underdog in the marquee West matchup.

The second round of the NBA playoffs always separates contenders from pretenders. Based on what we've seen so far, the Knicks and the Timberwolves look ready. The Spurs and Lakers face serious tests. And the Pistons and Cavaliers are about to find out if their Game 7 adrenaline carries over.

Four series. Sixteen games left before the Conference Finals. It is genuinely appointment television right now.
facebook.image_caption: Knicks 1-0. Wolves 1-0. Luka out. Two more Game 1s tonight. The 2026 Conference Semis are already delivering. Don't miss tonight — 7 PM and 8:30 PM ET.

article.headline: Knicks Rolling, Wolves Stealing, Luka Out: The 2026 NBA Conference Semifinals Are Already Historic
